title: phyllanthus vacciniifolius
intro: Phyllanthus vacciniifolius, also known as the Vaccinium-leaf Phyllanthus, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, particularly in Malaysia, Thailand, and Indonesia, characterized by its small, greenish-white flowers and red or purple berries. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, derived from its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The health benefits of Phyllanthus vacciniifolius include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and exhibit anti-diabetic and anti-cancer properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are used in various forms, including capsules, tablets, and topical creams.
